description: Stable machine-consumed transport, validation, storage, auth, and runtime failure codes.

codes.summary: The schema_id is not registered in the local protocol bundle.
The schema_id is known but the exact schema_version is not supported.
A digest reference uses a hash_alg value outside the MVP sha256 allowlist.
Communicating components do not share the same active local-session schema bundle version.
A streamed model response exceeded the idle timeout before the next event arrived.
The model gateway produced a terminal failure while handling the request.
The streamed request was cancelled before a successful terminal response was produced.
The broker rejected the request because local transport authentication credentials were unavailable or invalid.
The broker rejected the request during local admission/authentication before operation handling.
The broker rejected the request because request_id was missing or empty.
The broker rejected the request or response payload because it failed boundary schema validation.
The broker rejected the request because a base64 payload field was malformed.
The broker rejected the request because data_class was unsupported for the operation.
The broker rejected the request because the requested operation is not supported by this API surface.
The broker could not find the requested artifact for the provided digest.
The broker could not find the requested run for the provided run_id.
The broker could not find the requested approval for the provided approval_id.
The broker could not find the requested session for the provided session_id.
The broker rejected the request because encoded or decoded message size exceeded configured limits.
The broker rejected the request because JSON depth, array length, or object properties exceeded configured limits.
The broker rejected the request because in-flight request limits per client or lane were exceeded.
The broker rejected the request because per-client request rate exceeded configured limits for the current window.
The broker rejected the request due to policy-enforced runtime limits or flow controls.
The broker terminated a response stream because total streamed bytes exceeded configured limits.
The broker rejected or terminated the request because its deadline was exceeded.
The broker rejected approval handling because typed signed approval artifacts or required hash bindings were invalid.
The broker rejected the request because artifact range reads are not supported in MVP.
The broker rejected an untrusted runner checkpoint/result report because lifecycle transition validation failed closed.
Policy evaluation rejected a content-addressed input because payload bytes did not match the signed expected hash.
The requested backend acceleration capability is unavailable and launch failed closed.
MicroVM backend launch failed during hypervisor startup and no fallback backend was auto-selected.
Runtime image descriptor or signature evidence did not match required pinned launch identity.
The backend rejected attachment realization because the trusted attachment plan failed fail-closed validation.
Session establishment failed before backend activation due to handshake validation failure.
Session establishment failed because replay protection detected duplicated session identity material.
Session establishment or continuation failed because isolate binding evidence did not match the pinned session tuple.
The running backend became unresponsive and was terminated fail closed.
The backend watchdog terminated the runtime after heartbeat or progress timeout.
Launch failed closed because required runtime hardening controls were unavailable.
Launch failed closed because required workspace disk encryption or key protection was unavailable.
The runtime rejected automatic microVM-to-container fallback; container mode requires explicit flow semantics.
Container backend selection was rejected because explicit user opt-in flow was required.
